clinical_history: Persistent cough for 6 weeks; rule out pneumonia.
technique: Multi-detector CT of the chest with intravenous contrast, axial 1.25mm reformats, coronal and sagittal MPRs.
comparison: Prior CT chest 18 months ago, reviewed; relevant findings compared.
findings: Lungs and pleura: a single 4 mm solid pulmonary nodule in the right upper lobe (series 3, image 42); benign-appearing fissural calcification on the left. No pleural effusion. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Heart and great vessels: normal cardiac silhouette; no pericardial effusion. Upper abdomen: liver, spleen, adrenals unremarkable; no incidental liver lesion. Bones: stable degenerative changes thoracic spine, otherwise unremarkable.
impression: 1. 4 mm solid pulmonary nodule, right upper lobe, low-risk patient. Per Fleischner 2017, no routine follow-up indicated. 2. No acute pulmonary or cardiac findings. 3. Stable interval compared to prior. Recommendation: Clinical correlation; no imaging follow-up required at this time.
